summary: Tested PosePerfect AI backend APIs and frontend UI. Backend: 14/14 pytest cases passed - root endpoint, /api/poses with mood/scene filtering, pose-by-id, status CRUD, no Mongo _id leakage. Frontend: page loads with glassmorphic header, all 3 mood badges + 4 scene selectors render and toggle selection state, mood/scene change triggers /api/poses fetch, Start Camera button is clickable, MediaPipe initializes ('Graph successfully started running' logged twice), camera error toast displayed as expected (no device in test env).

minor.issue: Returns 200 with empty stub object instead of 404 when pose not found

issues: Error toast 'Requested device not found' visually overlaps the Start Camera button center bottom - consider moving toast to top or hiding on idle re-click

action_items: Optional: Return 404 for unknown pose_id in GET /api/poses/{pose_id} instead of stub object
Optional: Reposition camera error toast so it does not overlap Start Camera button
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: get_pose_by_id returns a fake stub when not found - should return HTTPException 404 for proper REST semantics
server.py seed_poses runs only when collection is empty; if seed schema changes, no migration path
PoseDirector.jsx: useEffect refetches on every selectedMood/selectedScene change without debounce - fine here, just noting
AlignmentIndicator: alignmentScore formula (1 - avgDistance*5)*100 is heuristic; document or make threshold configurable
No backend tests existed prior - added comprehensive test_poses_api.py with 14 cases including parameterized mood/scene filters
seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 7 trending poses on startup; verified present in DB via API
context_for_next_testing_agent: Camera-dependent flows (WireframeOverlay rendering, pose navigation prev/next buttons, AlignmentIndicator, capture-button) cannot be tested in this env because getUserMedia fails with NotFoundError. These elements only render when cameraActive && latestPose is true. To test them, mock MediaPipe pose results or inject a fake video stream. Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_poses_api.py.
